page: Microsoft Azure E64s_v7 is an Esv7-family virtual machine configured with 64 vCPUs and 512.0 GB of RAM, yielding an 8.0 GB per vCPU ratio. Powered by an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor with 32 physical cores and 2 threads per core, this x86_64 architecture instance features a dedicated CPU allocation. The processor includes a 480 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX).

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core capabilities, with stress-ng multi-core and PassMark CPU scores placing in the top 25%. Memory bandwidth is also highly rated, with read-only small block cached bandwidth reaching 4689.08 GB/sec and large block uncached write bandwidth at 147.79 GB/sec. Single-core performance is moderate, with stress-ng single-core and bzip3 single-threaded decompression scoring in the middle 50%.

A notable hardware tradeoff is the absence of local storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met via external Premium Storage. This configuration offers high resource density for memory-bound workloads, providing qualitative cost efficiency for applications requiring large memory footprints without the overhead of local NVMe drives.

The instance is suited for memory-intensive workloads, including database operations, Redis deployments, and multi-threaded data processing. This is supported by strong benchmark results in PassMark database operations and Redis SET operations, which recorded 105.13 million operations per second.
description: Microsoft Azure E64s_v7 is an Esv7-family virtual machine featuring 64 vCPUs and 512.0 GB of RAM, establishing a memory-optimized 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. Powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor with 32 physical cores and a 480 MB L3 cache, the instance supports AVX-512 and Intel AMX instructions. Benchmarks show strong multi-core CPU performance and high memory bandwidth, alongside average single-core metrics. The server lacks local storage, requiring external Premium Storage. This hardware profile is qualitatively cost-effective for memory-intensive applications that do not require local NVMe drives. It is well-suited for database operations, Redis caching, and multi-threaded data processing, as evidenced by its strong performance in Redis SET operations and PassMark database benchmarks.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E64s_v7 is an Esv7 virtual machine with 64 vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor. It offers strong multi-core performance and is optimized for memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E64s_v7 is a memory-optimized VM with 64 vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon CPU, designed for database and multi-threaded workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring sixty-four vCPUs, half a terabyte of RAM, and advanced Intel matrix acceleration for demanding database workloads.
bullet_points: 64 vCPUs and 512.0 GB of RAM providing a 1:8 memory-to-vCPU ratio
Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor with 32 physical cores and 480 MB L3 cache
Strong multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth benchmark performance
No local storage, requiring external Premium Storage solutions
Suited for database operations, Redis caching, and multi-threaded processing
